text: Abstract The ortho -substituted phenyl ring is a fundamental structure in chemistry. Hundreds of drugs and agrochemicals contain an ortho -substituted phenyl ring. Although considerable efforts have been made over the last ten years to replace the aromatic phenyl ring with saturated structures (bioisosteres) in order to obtain patentable new structures, the majority of research efforts are focused on para -substituted structures. As an example of the feasibility of replacing ortho -substituted phenyl rings with stable saturated structures, we have synthesized a series of 2-oxabicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes derivatives, which possess superior properties compared to the ortho -substituted phenyl ring. X-ray analysis revealed that these cyclic derivatives and the ortho -substituted phenyl ring possess similar spatial features. The marketed agrochemicals fluxapyroxad (BASF) and boscalid (BASF) exhibited good bioactivity when their ortho -substituted phenyl ring was replaced with a 2-oxabicyclo[2.1.1]hexane derivative, which showed improved aqueous solubility, lower lipophilicity and retain bioactivity. Our results demonstrate the possibility of replacing ortho -substituted phenyl rings in bioactive compounds with saturated bioisosteres in medicinal chemistry and agrochemistry.
